workflow.name: Create Next Story
workflow.critical: Create a comprehensive story file that gives the dev agent EVERYTHING needed for flawless implementation
EXHAUSTIVE ANALYSIS — thoroughly analyze artifacts, do NOT skim
Prevent common LLM mistakes: reinventing wheels, wrong libraries, wrong file locations, vague implementations
ZERO USER INTERVENTION except for initial epic/story selection

check.if: story_path or epic-story number provided by user

step.action: Find FIRST story with status "backlog" (pattern: N-N-name, NOT epic-X)

check.output: Toutes les stories sont deja creees ou en cours. Lance /pm pour voir le dashboard.

step.action: Extract epic_num, story_num, story_key from found entry

check.ask: Story file existe deja. [skip] passer a la suivante ou [overwrite] ecraser?

check.action: Update epic-N status: backlog → in-progress in sprint-status.yaml

step.critical: This is where you prevent future developer mistakes — be thorough!
step.action: Load epic file from planning artifacts (epic-{{epic_num}}*.md)
Extract: epic objectives, ALL stories for cross-story context, our story's requirements, ACs, dependencies
step.critical: Epic ACs = functional source of truth (WHAT must exist). Design = visual guide (HOW it looks). If the design omits a feature required by the epic/ACs, the feature is STILL required — infer UI from conventions.

check.if: story has .pen Design Reference in epic
check.action: Pencil MCP: batch_get to read .pen nodes
Pencil MCP: get_screenshot for visual reference
Pencil MCP: snapshot_layout for exact measurements
Extract into Dev Notes: colors (hex → semantic tokens), typography (px), spacing (px), layout specs
Add "Design Tokens Reference" subsection mapping .pen values → Tailwind classes
Add: "VISUAL VALIDATION REQUIRED: Playwright MCP screenshot after implementation"
Find and load previous story file in same epic
Extract: learnings, review feedback, code patterns established, problems encountered

step.action: Load architecture doc (if exists) — extract story-relevant patterns: tech stack, code structure, API patterns, DB schemas, security, testing standards
Get last 5 commit titles for recent work patterns
Analyze: files modified, conventions used, dependencies added
Load learnings.xml — integrate relevant antipatterns into story Dev Notes as warnings

Populate sections: - Story: user story from epic - Context & Motivation: business value + technical context - ACs: BDD format from epic (Given/When/Then) - Tasks/Subtasks: detailed implementation steps with AC references - Dev Notes: architecture requirements, key files, constraints, design tokens, previous story learnings, relevant antipatterns from learnings.xml
step.critical: Before finalizing tasks, run these two validations:
step.action: AC → UI path check: For each operation required by the ACs (every CRUD verb, every user action), verify that the generated tasks include a UI element that triggers it. If missing: add a task for the missing UI element, using standard conventions (e.g., delete button in list rows, edit icon, etc.).

Backend ↔ Frontend cross-check: List every Server Action / API route in the backend tasks. For each one, confirm a frontend task exists that calls it. If a Server Action has no UI trigger: add the missing frontend task. Log any additions in Dev Notes as "Added by functional completeness check — not in design".

step.output: **Story {{story_key}} creee!** Fichier: {{story_path}} Status: ready-for-dev Prochaine etape: lance /dev pour implementer.
